fire+water.fact: geysers are natural steam engines with no moving parts — old faithful has kept a rough eruption schedule for over a century.

earth+water.fact: mudskippers are fish that walk on mud — they can drown if they're held underwater too long.

air+water.fact: fog is just a cloud that touches the ground — same physics, lower altitude.

earth+fire.fact: runny basaltic lava can flow faster than you can run — nyiragongo's 2002 flows hit ~60 km/h.

air+fire.fact: smoke rings are vortex rings — dolphins make the same shape underwater by blowing bubble rings for fun.

air+earth.fact: part of your household dust is cosmic — about 5,000 tonnes of micrometeorite dust fall to earth every year.

water+water.fact: we have better high-resolution maps of mars than of our own ocean floor.

fire+fire.fact: the sun is 99.86% of the solar system's mass — everything else is a rounding error.

earth+earth.fact: everest gets about 4 mm taller every year as india keeps pushing into asia.

air+air.fact: all wind is solar-powered — uneven heating of the earth's surface is what drives it.

air+steam.fact: an average cumulus cloud weighs around a million pounds — it floats because that mass is spread over a huge volume.

cloud+cloud.fact: a thunderstorm can hold more energy than an atomic bomb — most of it spent lifting water.

cloud+water.fact: raindrops aren't teardrop-shaped — small ones are spheres and big ones flatten like hamburger buns.

rain+sun.fact: no two people ever see the same rainbow — it depends on the exact position of your eyes.

fire+mud.fact: fired clay bricks from 5,000 years ago are still standing — one of the oldest manufactured materials we have.

lava+water.fact: obsidian blades can be sharper than steel scalpels — some surgeons have actually used them.

earth+storm.fact: lightning strikes earth about 100 times every second — roughly 8 million bolts a day.

lightning+ocean.fact: in 1952 the miller-urey experiment zapped a 'primordial soup' with sparks and made amino acids — building blocks of life.

life+water.fact: fish sense the world through a lateral line — a pressure-sensing organ that feels the water move.

fish+fish.fact: a fish school has no leader — every fish follows a few simple local rules, and the swirl emerges on its own. (that's what the fish on this page are doing.)

earth+life.fact: experiments suggest plant roots can grow toward the sound of running water — even with no moisture present.

earth+plant.fact: sharks are older than trees — sharks go back ~450 million years, trees only ~385 million.

tree+tree.fact: forest trees trade nutrients through underground fungal networks — nicknamed the wood wide web.

air+life.fact: birds are living dinosaurs — a hummingbird and a t. rex share a common ancestor.

bird+ocean.fact: emperor penguins can dive over 500 metres deep and hold their breath for around 20 minutes.

fire+life.fact: firefly light is nearly 100% efficient — almost no energy is wasted as heat, unlike a lightbulb.

life+life.fact: you replace roughly 330 billion cells every day — about 1% of your body, daily.

earth+sun.fact: the sahara feeds the amazon — its dust blows across the atlantic and fertilizes the rainforest.

air+desert.fact: some sand dunes 'sing' — avalanching grains hum loud enough to hear from kilometres away.

ocean+wind.fact: the tallest wave ever recorded was 524 metres — a 1958 megatsunami in lituya bay, alaska.

lava+ocean.fact: the island of surtsey didn't exist before 1963 — it erupted out of the sea near iceland and is still there.

air+sun.fact: the sky is blue because air scatters blue light more than red — sunsets are red for the same reason, just a longer path.

sun+sun.fact: there are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of earth's beaches.

star+star.fact: the milky way and andromeda will merge in ~4.5 billion years — and almost no stars will actually collide.

earth+star.fact: most shooting stars are the size of a grain of sand, burning up at around 60 km per second.

fish+lightning.fact: electric eels deliver shocks up to 600 volts — and they aren't eels, they're knifefish.

air+fish.fact: flying fish can glide 400 metres in one go — longer than four football fields.

life+mud.fact: earthworms have five pairs of heart-like structures pumping their blood.

plant+sun.fact: a single sunflower head is actually up to 2,000 tiny flowers packed in a spiral.

flower+life.fact: one honeybee makes about 1/12 of a teaspoon of honey in its entire lifetime.

earth+steam.fact: geysers need a rare plumbing accident of heat, water, and a pressure-tight rock throat — fewer than a thousand exist on earth.
